prompt: Which request is an anti-pattern for an autonomous coding agent?

options.text: Update one dependency and run the specified regression tests
Fix every security problem in the organization and merge whatever is needed
Add a documented validation rule to one service
Generate a migration plan without changing code

explanation: The request is unbounded, crosses organizational and repository boundaries, lacks success criteria, and authorizes merging without review. Agent work should have explicit scope, outputs, constraints, and validation.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 agent architecture objectives

prompt: Which two details are most important when validating an agent's implementation plan? Select two.

options.text: The files, dependencies, and systems the plan expects to change
The model's preferred writing style
The tests, checks, and rollback conditions that will validate the change
The number of tokens used to produce the plan

explanation: Plan validation should confirm scope and dependencies, then verify that the proposed work has concrete validation and recovery conditions. Style and token count do not establish that the plan is safe or executable.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 planning and execution objectives

prompt: A workflow must ensure that a security-sensitive plan is approved before any repository changes occur. Which design is strongest?

options.text: Tell the implementation agent to remember not to edit early
Use separate planning and execution stages, with execution dependent on an approval result
Let the agent edit first and describe the plan in the pull request
Remove write permissions only after the first commit

explanation: A separate planning stage produces a reviewable artifact, while a dependency or approval gate prevents the state-changing stage from starting until the plan is accepted.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 planning and execution objectives

prompt: Which task can usually be assigned the highest autonomy with the least human intervention?

options.text: Summarize documentation and propose nonbinding edits on an isolated branch
Change production firewall rules
Approve a compliance exception
Delete customer data after an ambiguous request

explanation: Read-oriented or easily reviewable branch work has limited blast radius and can receive more autonomy. Production, compliance, and destructive actions require stronger controls and human judgment.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 observability and autonomy objectives

prompt: Which two choices preserve delivery speed while retaining meaningful human control? Select two.

options.text: Automatically run tests and scans before requesting review
Require manual approval before every read-only tool call
Escalate only when a material risk threshold or irreversible action is reached
Allow the agent to bypass controls whenever a deadline is near

explanation: Automated evidence removes low-value waiting, while risk-based escalation reserves human attention for decisions where judgment materially reduces risk. Universal approvals slow delivery without improving safety.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 observability and autonomy objectives
